species: Candidatus Berkelbacteria (uncultured CPR bacterium; ASV-level taxon, Parcubacteria-related lineage)

growth_records.strain: not isolated (ASV from groundwater 16S rRNA amplicon dataset)
growth_records.medium: Oligotrophic groundwater microcosm (specific treatment for this value not identified in text)
growth_records.cite: Gabashvili et al. Growth of candidate phyla radiation bacteria in groundwater incubations reveals widespread adaptations to oxic conditions. Microbiome (2025). DOI:10.1186/s40168-025-02244-1. [PMC12577380]
growth_records.snippet: The faster-growing members of Cand. Saccharimonadia and Cand. Berkelbacteria only reached doubling times of 6 and 4 days, respectively.
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (4 days = 96 h) directly reported in text for fastest-growing Berkelbacteria ASVs. growth_rate_per_h calculated as mu=ln2/96h=0.00722 h-1. Growth rates were calculated by authors via linear regression of ln(absolute 16S rRNA gene copies, qPCR total x relative abundance) vs incubation time (slope=k, exponential growth model). Organism identity is ASV/candidate-phylum level only in a mixed community groundwater incubation, not an axenic isolate; incubation at 15C in the dark with rotational shaking per general Methods; exact treatment/medium supplement for this specific value not stated.
